segments.text: PART 3
KIERAN: So Jane - you'll be off to Denmark soon to do your work placement.
segments.translation: 基兰：简，你很快就要去丹麦实习了。

segments.text: JANE: Yes, I'm really looking forward to it and I've just started packing up all my books to put in storage.
segments.translation: 简：是的，我真的很期待，我刚开始收拾我所有的书，准备放进仓库。

segments.text: KIERAN: Well, I hope they don't get spoilt.
segments.translation: 基兰：希望它们不会被弄坏。

segments.text: JANE: It's OK - my grandfather works in a bookshop and he told me how to pack them.
segments.translation: 简：没事的，我爷爷在书店工作，他告诉我怎么打包。

segments.text: KIERAN: Oh, that's helpful.
segments.translation: 基兰：这很有用。

segments.text: JANE: He says you have to support the spine otherwise the paper can come away from the cover.
segments.translation: 简：他说必须支撑住书脊，否则纸张会从封面上脱落。

segments.text: KIERAN: Yeah - that's obvious.
segments.translation: 基兰：是的，很显然。

segments.text: JANE: He also told me to pack them flat in the box not on their side - again because they can bend and if you leave them like that for, say, a year, it's quite hard to get them back to their normal shape.
segments.translation: 简：他还告诉我要把书平放在盒子里，不要侧放，因为书会弯曲，如果你把书这样放一年，就很难让它们恢复正常形状了。

segments.text: KIERAN: Well, it's pretty clear that ruins them, but a lot of people just can't be bothered to protect their books.
segments.translation: 基兰：很明显这会毁了他们，但很多人就是懒得保护他们的书。

segments.text: JANE: He always says it's such a shame that publishers don't use better-quality paper.
segments.translation: 简: 爷爷总是说，出版商不使用质量更好的纸张真是太可惜了。

segments.text: KIERAN: It's the acid in the paper that causes the problem, isn't it?
segments.translation: 基兰：纸张里的酸性物质才是问题所在，不是吗？

segments.text: JANE: Yeah - that's why old books go yellow. You know some of the books my grandfather's given me are like that already.
segments.translation: 简：是啊，这就是为什么旧书会变黄。你知道我爷爷给我的一些书就已经是这样了。

segments.text: KIERAN: Oh . . .
segments.translation: 基兰：天啊……

segments.text: JANE: I should dump them really if they're going to deteriorate further, but I'd feel bad.
segments.translation: 简：如果这些书继续变黄的话，我真应该把它们扔了，但是又觉得很可惜。

segments.text: They'll always remind me of him. He's quite a collector, you know.
segments.translation: 这些书总是让我想起我爷爷，他是个收藏家。

segments.text: KIERAN: Well, if they're important to you . . .
segments.translation: 基兰：是的，如果它们对你很重要的话. . .

segments.text: JANE: Yeah - I'd regret just throwing them away.
segments.translation: 简：是啊，要是把它们扔掉我会后悔的。

segments.text: KIERAN: You know, maybe it's because I was taught to treasure books . . . but I hate seeing students force open the pages - of paperbacks. They press so hard they end up breaking the spine.
segments.translation: 基兰：也许是因为我从小就被教育要爱惜书本吧，但我最讨厌看到学生们用力撕开平装书的书页，他们用力过猛，最后把书脊都压断了。

segments.text: JANE: I know, but unfortunately, paperbacks aren't designed to last a long time and people know that. Hardbacks aren't quite as weak.
segments.translation: 简：我知道，但不幸的是，平装书的设计并不耐用，人们也知道这一点。精装书就没那么脆弱了。

segments.text: KIERAN: Yeah, they're different, I suppose. But I still don't think people value hardbacks like they used to.
segments.translation: 基兰：是的，我想它们是不一样的。但是我仍然认为人们不像以前那样看重精装书。

segments.text: JANE: Well, they aren't decorative, are they, like other objects. Plus, nowadays, people don't keep them out on shelves as much as they used to.
segments.translation: 简：精装书不像其他物品那样具有装饰性，不是吗？另外，现在人们不再像以前那样把精装书放在书架上了。

segments.text: KIERAN: That's such a pity. When I visit someone - if they have, say, a colourful book on a table, it's the first thing I'm drawn to.
segments.translation: 基兰：真遗憾。我去拜访别人时，如果他们的桌子上摆着一本色彩缤纷的书，我首先就会被它吸引。

segments.text: JANE: I agree - and book covers can be a work of art in themselves. Some are really eye-catching.
segments.translation: 简：我同意，书的封面本身就是一件艺术品。有些封面真的很引人注目。

segments.text: KIERAN: I've always been taught to handle books carefully. If you watch someone take a book off a shelf, well, they usually do it wrong.
segments.translation: 基兰：我一直被教导要小心处理书籍。如果你看到有人从书架上取下一本书，那么，通常他们取书的方式是错的。

segments.text: JANE: Ah, my grandfather says, you should put your hand right over the top of the book . . . or if you can't do that, pull the other books on the shelf aside so that you can hold the whole cover.
segments.translation: 简：我爷爷说，应该把手放在书的正上方，或者如果做不到，把书架上的其他书拉到一边，这样就可以把整个封面都拿起来。

segments.text: KIERAN: When did you learn all this?
segments.translation: 基兰：你什么时候学会这些的？

segments.text: JANE: He watched me pull a heavy book off the shelf when I was small, and it fell on the floor and broke apart.
segments.translation: 简：小时候，他看着我从书架上取下一本很重的书，书掉在地上摔散架了。

segments.text: KIERAN: Oh dear!
segments.translation: 基兰：天哪！

segments.text: JANE: I can still remember it!
segments.translation: 简：我一直记到现在！

segments.text: KIERAN: You know what I really like?
segments.translation: 基兰：你知道我最喜欢什么吗？

segments.text: JANE: What?
segments.translation: 简：是什么？

segments.text: KIERAN: The smell of new books.
segments.translation: 基兰：新书的味道。

segments.text: JANE: Me too.
segments.translation: 简：我也是。

segments.text: KIERAN: My parents used to laugh at me when I was a kid because I loved putting books up to my nose. Almost as much as reading them!
segments.translation: 基兰：小时候，我父母常常嘲笑我，因为我喜欢把书放在鼻子上。几乎和我喜欢读书一样不相上下！

segments.text: JANE: New books aren't cheap, though, are they?
segments.translation: 简: 不过新书并不便宜，不是吗？

segments.text: KIERAN: I guess we're lucky we can buy them.
segments.translation: 基兰：我想我们能买到它们已经很幸运了。

segments.text: JANE: My grandfather stocks second-hand books as well as new ones and they don't smell quite as good.
segments.translation: 简: 我爷爷存放二手书和新书，它们闻起来没有那么好。

segments.text: KIERAN: I'd love to have a bookshop like your grandfather. What's it like?
segments.translation: 基兰：我很想像你爷爷那样开一家书店。它是什么样的？

segments.text: JANE: Well, it's quite big - it's got two floors and an attic, and he stocks all kinds of books really.
segments.translation: 简：嗯，挺大的，有两层楼，还有阁楼，什么书都有。

segments.text: KIERAN: I guess he treasures things like first editions and other rare books.
segments.translation: 基兰：我猜他珍藏的有初版书和其他稀有书籍。

segments.text: JANE: Yeah - you might think he'd keep those in the attic or somewhere.
segments.translation: 简：是啊，你可能以为他会把那些书放在阁楼或什么地方。

segments.text: KIERAN: . . . so they'd be hidden?
segments.translation: 基兰：所以这些书会被藏起来吗？

segments.text: JANE: Yeah. But he likes people to know that he has them.
segments.translation: 简: 是的。但是他又喜欢让人们知道他拥有他们。

segments.text: So, he puts them out in the shop but makes sure you need a ladder to get them.
segments.translation: 所以他把这些书就放在店里，但确保你需要梯子才能拿到它们

segments.text: KIERAN: Right. That would prevent any thefts!
segments.translation: 基兰：没错，这样就不会被偷了。

segments.text: JANE: Uhuh.
segments.translation: 简：嗯。

segments.text: KIERAN: Does he stock books for children?
segments.translation: 基兰：他有儿童读物吗？

segments.text: JANE: He does. He particularly likes to encourage kids to read; he always says that he used to sit under the stairs as a child with a pile of books and read them all.
segments.translation: 简：是的。他特别喜欢鼓励孩子们读书，他总是说他小时候常常坐在楼梯下面，手里拿着一堆书，把它们全部读完。

segments.text: KIERAN: Is that where he keeps them, then?
segments.translation: 基兰：那他就把书放在那里吗？

segments.text: JANE: Not exactly - he's got a dedicated area on the ground floor with cushions so that parents can enter with their toddlers, go there and spend some time reading to them.
segments.translation: 简：不完全是--他在一楼有一个专门的区域，铺上软垫，这样家长们就可以带着他们的孩子进去，到那里花一些时间给他们读书。

segments.text: KIERAN: Oh cool.
segments.translation: 基兰：太酷了。

segments.text: JANE: And then there's a place for pushchairs by the front door. And a café if anyone needs refreshments.
segments.translation: 简: 前门还有个地方可以放手推车。如果有人需要点心，还可以去咖啡馆。

segments.text: KIERAN: That's good to know.
segments.translation: 基兰：听起来很不错。

segments.text: JANE: As I said, it's a big shop and there's a storage area out the back as well.
segments.translation: 简：正如我所说，这是一家大店铺，后面还有一个储藏室。

segments.text: KIERAN: Oh, what does he keep there? Books he wants to throw away?
segments.translation: 基兰：哦，他在哪里都放了什么？ 他想扔掉的书？

segments.text: JANE: He hardly ever throws anything away - he just leaves unwanted books by the front door for customers to take.
segments.translation: 简：他几乎不扔任何东西--他只是把不想要的书放在前门，让顾客拿走。

segments.text: KIERAN: Well, that's very nice.
segments.translation: 基兰：嗯，真不错。

segments.text: JANE: Yeah - and books people or institutions have requested, they all go at the far end.
segments.translation: 简：是的，人们或机构要求的书籍都放在最里面。

segments.text: KIERAN: Oh.
segments.translation: 基兰：哦。

segments.text: JANE: He thinks it's best to keep these out of the main shopping area as they're boxed and new.
segments.translation: 简：他认为这些书最好不要放在主要购物区，因为它们都是装箱的新书。

segments.text: KIERAN: Did you get your coursebooks from him?
segments.translation: 基兰：你的课本是从他那里拿的吗？

segments.text: JANE: Naturally. He stocks books for a lot of the colleges.
segments.translation: 简：那是自然。他为很多大学储备书籍。

segments.text: He used to keep these books on the first floor, but now there's a new university in my hometown, he's moved them downstairs to attract the students.
segments.translation: 他以前把这些书放在一楼，但现在我家乡新开了一所大学，他就把书搬到楼下，以吸引学生。

segments.text: They're actually part of the coffee shop, on low shelves all around it.
segments.translation: 它们实际上是咖啡店的一部分，在咖啡店周围低矮的架子上。

segments.text: KIERAN: Pretty central then. You'll have to take me there some time!
segments.translation: 基兰：那就是中心了，你得找个时间带我去那里！
